Course Title: AI on Edge Devices: Security Trade-Offs
Category: Technology & Telecommunications: Information Technology, Science & Technology, Software, Hardware, Telecom
Who is this for: Product managers, software developers, IT managers, technology analysts, IoT engineers, mobile app developers, system architects
Team or Dept this is for: IT, Product Development, Engineering, Operations, Technology Strategy
Description: Running models on devices vs centralised systems.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how computers and networks function, along with a general interest in artificial intelligence concepts, is all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key differences between running AI on edge devices and centralised systems, and explain the core security trade-offs involved in each approach. You will also apply practical frameworks to evaluate when edge deployment is appropriate, and recognise the privacy, latency, and resource considerations that influence real-world design decisions.
